user_query:
I'm jumping into a messy weekly data ops handoff and need you to normalize three inputs and produce clean artifacts for a quick report. Read the files under input/:
- input/sales.csv (columns: order_id, product, units_sold, revenue)
- input/subscribers.txt (email list with duplicates)
- input/events.json (JSON array of event objects with keys: event, timestamp, user)

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
1) output/sales_rank.txt — Rank ALL rows in input/sales.csv by revenue (numeric, descending). Keep the original header context in the output (if your approach prints a header and a dashed separator, preserve them). Rank 1 must be the highest revenue row. If two rows have the same revenue, maintain their original file order (stable tie-break). Include the full ranked list.
2) output/sales_top3.txt — Show the top 3 rows by revenue (numeric, descending) from input/sales.csv. Preserve the original header and any dashed separator if present. Ties must remain in original order.
3) output/subscribers_dedup.txt — Deduplicate input/subscribers.txt preserving the first occurrence order. Treat duplicates as exact string matches (case-sensitive). Output exactly one email per line.
4) output/subscribers_freq.txt — Produce a frequency table of input/subscribers.txt showing how many times each exact line appears. Include a simple header row for readability.
5) output/events_sorted.json — Sort input/events.json by the "timestamp" field ascending and output a valid JSON array.
6) output/README.md — A brief 8–20 line summary of what you did, including:
   - Which columns/keys were used and why
   - How you ensured numeric sorting for revenue
   - Your tie-break rule for equal revenues
   - Deduplication rule (exact match, case-sensitive) and why
   - Any quick validation checks you performed (e.g., counts before/after)

Rules:
- Use only the provided files under input/ and write outputs under output/.
- Do not modify any files under input/.
- Keep outputs deterministic and machine-checkable.
- Avoid adding extra commentary inside the data outputs beyond any standard header/separator lines your method emits.
